epic.name: Webhook Provider Abstraction & Multi-Source Ingestion
epic.objective: Create a WebhookProvider interface that normalizes alerts from any monitoring source, refactor existing Prometheus ingestion to use it, and implement Grafana, CloudWatch, and Generic webhook providers
epic.definition_of_done: All four webhook endpoints accept payloads, store alerts with correct source, and auto-create incidents. Adding a new source requires only implementing the WebhookProvider interface.
epic.notes: v0.1 built Prometheus webhook with ProcessAlertmanagerPayload() tightly coupled to Alertmanager format. This epic decouples the pipeline by introducing NormalizedAlert as the internal interchange format. The existing Prometheus flow is refactored but behavior remains identical (backwards compatible).

tasks.name: Design WebhookProvider interface and NormalizedAlert type
tasks.description: Create the core abstraction that all webhook sources will implement. NormalizedAlert is the canonical internal representation that the AlertService processes, regardless of source.
acceptance_criteria: WebhookProvider interface defined with ParsePayload(body []byte) ([]NormalizedAlert, error)
WebhookProvider interface includes ValidatePayload(body []byte, headers http.Header) error for signature/auth checks
WebhookProvider interface includes Source() string returning the source identifier

NormalizedAlert struct defined with: ExternalID, Source, Status, Severity, Title, Description, Labels (map[string]string), Annotations (map[string]string), RawPayload (json.RawMessage), StartedAt, EndedAt
NormalizedAlert includes a Fingerprint() method deriving deduplication key from source + external_id
Types defined in backend/internal/models/webhooks/provider.go
Comprehensive godoc comments on interface contract
tasks.implementation_notes: This is the keystone type — every subsequent task depends on it. Keep NormalizedAlert flat and simple; source-specific metadata goes in Labels/Annotations/RawPayload.

tasks.name: Refactor AlertService to accept NormalizedAlerts
tasks.description: Add a generic ProcessNormalizedAlerts() method to AlertService that handles any source. The existing ProcessAlertmanagerPayload() delegates to this method after Prometheus-specific parsing.
acceptance_criteria: AlertService interface gains ProcessNormalizedAlerts(alerts []NormalizedAlert) (*ProcessingResult, error)
ProcessNormalizedAlerts handles: alert creation/update, deduplication, incident auto-creation
Existing ProcessAlertmanagerPayload() refactored to: parse → normalize → call ProcessNormalizedAlerts()
All existing Prometheus webhook tests continue to pass without modification
ProcessingResult includes per-source counts: {source: string, created: int, updated: int, incidents_created: int}
Deduplication logic unchanged: (source, external_id) composite key
tasks.implementation_notes: Critical refactor — must not break existing Prometheus flow. Run full test suite after every change. The normalizeAlert() private method is extracted and moved to the Prometheus provider.

tasks.name: Refactor Prometheus webhook to WebhookProvider pattern
tasks.description: Convert the existing Prometheus-specific parsing into a PrometheusProvider that implements WebhookProvider. This is a pure refactor — behavior must be identical.
acceptance_criteria: PrometheusProvider struct implements WebhookProvider interface
PrometheusProvider.ParsePayload() converts AlertmanagerPayload → []NormalizedAlert
PrometheusProvider.ValidatePayload() is a no-op (Prometheus uses webhook URL secrecy, no signing)
PrometheusProvider.Source() returns 'prometheus'
Existing prometheus_webhook.go handler delegates to generic webhook handler with PrometheusProvider
All existing integration tests pass without modification
Provider defined in backend/internal/models/webhooks/prometheus.go (extends existing file)
No behavior change — this is a structural refactor only
tasks.implementation_notes: Move normalizeAlert() logic from alert_service.go into PrometheusProvider.ParsePayload(). The handler becomes a thin wrapper: parse JSON → provider.ParsePayload() → alertService.ProcessNormalizedAlerts().

tasks.name: Implement Grafana webhook provider
tasks.description: Create a GrafanaProvider that handles Grafana Alerting webhook payloads (Grafana 9+ Unified Alerting format) and normalizes them into NormalizedAlerts
acceptance_criteria: GrafanaProvider implements WebhookProvider interface
Handles Grafana Unified Alerting webhook format (POST JSON with alerts array)
Maps Grafana fields: title→Title, message→Description, status (alerting/resolved/no_data)→Status
Maps Grafana severity: alerting→warning (default), severity label override if present
ExternalID derived from Grafana alert fingerprint or orgId+dashboardId+panelId+alertId combo
Labels extracted from Grafana labels object, annotations from annotations object
RawPayload stores complete Grafana webhook JSON
POST /api/v1/webhooks/grafana endpoint registered and functional
Grafana 'no_data' state mapped to severity='info' (no incident auto-created)
Provider defined in backend/internal/models/webhooks/grafana.go
tasks.implementation_notes: Grafana Unified Alerting (v9+) uses a different webhook format than legacy Grafana alerts. Focus on the modern format. Grafana payloads include orgId, dashboardId, panelId which together form a unique alert identity.

tasks.name: Implement CloudWatch webhook provider (SNS)
tasks.description: Create a CloudWatchProvider that handles AWS CloudWatch Alarm notifications delivered via SNS HTTP/HTTPS subscriptions
acceptance_criteria: CloudWatchProvider implements WebhookProvider interface
Handles SNS notification wrapper: extracts CloudWatch alarm payload from SNS Message field
Handles SNS SubscriptionConfirmation messages by auto-confirming (GET SubscribeURL)
Maps CloudWatch fields: AlarmName→Title, AlarmDescription+StateChangeReason→Description
Maps CloudWatch states: ALARM→firing, OK→resolved, INSUFFICIENT_DATA→firing with severity=info
Maps trigger severity from ComparisonOperator and Threshold context
ExternalID derived from AlarmArn (globally unique in AWS)
Labels include: region, namespace, metric_name, dimensions
RawPayload stores complete SNS envelope with CloudWatch payload
POST /api/v1/webhooks/cloudwatch endpoint registered and functional
ValidatePayload() verifies SNS message signature using AWS certificate
Provider defined in backend/internal/models/webhooks/cloudwatch.go
tasks.implementation_notes: CloudWatch alarms arrive wrapped in SNS notifications. Must handle two message types: SubscriptionConfirmation (one-time setup) and Notification (alarm state change). SNS signature verification is important for security — AWS publishes the signing certificate URL in the message.

tasks.name: Implement Generic webhook provider
tasks.description: Create a GenericProvider that accepts a simplified, well-documented JSON schema for any monitoring tool that doesn't have a dedicated provider
acceptance_criteria: GenericProvider implements WebhookProvider interface
Accepts a documented JSON schema: {alerts: [{title, description, severity, status, external_id, labels, started_at}]}
All fields optional except title; defaults: severity=warning, status=firing, external_id=SHA256(title+labels)
If external_id not provided, auto-generated from SHA256 hash of title + sorted labels for deduplication
ValidatePayload() supports optional HMAC-SHA256 signature via X-Webhook-Secret header
POST /api/v1/webhooks/generic endpoint registered and functional
Comprehensive documentation of the generic schema published at GET /api/v1/webhooks/generic/schema
Provider defined in backend/internal/models/webhooks/generic.go
Example payloads documented for Datadog, New Relic, custom scripts
tasks.implementation_notes: The generic webhook is the 'escape hatch' for any monitoring tool. Keep the schema simple and well-documented. Users can use curl or any HTTP client to send alerts. The schema endpoint helps with discoverability.

tasks.name: Create unified webhook handler and register all routes
tasks.description: Create a generic WebhookHandler that works with any WebhookProvider, eliminating per-source handler boilerplate. Register all four webhook routes.
acceptance_criteria: GenericWebhookHandler struct wraps: WebhookProvider + AlertService
Handler flow: ReadBody → ValidatePayload → ParsePayload → ProcessNormalizedAlerts → Response
Consistent error responses across all providers (400 for parse errors, 401 for auth failures, 500 for internal)
Consistent success response: {received: N, created: N, updated: N, incidents_created: N, source: string}
All four routes registered in routes.go: /prometheus, /grafana, /cloudwatch, /generic
Request logging middleware: source, alert_count, processing_time_ms, status_code
Prometheus metrics updated: openincident_webhooks_received_total{source=...}
Max payload size enforced per provider (default 1MB, configurable)
tasks.implementation_notes: This handler eliminates the per-source handler files. The Prometheus-specific handler can be replaced by the generic handler wired with PrometheusProvider. Keep the existing /prometheus URL — do not break existing Alertmanager configs.

tasks.name: Integration tests for all webhook providers
tasks.description: Comprehensive integration tests for Grafana, CloudWatch, and Generic webhook endpoints using realistic payload samples
acceptance_criteria: Test suite for each provider: Grafana, CloudWatch, Generic
Each suite tests: valid payload → alert created + incident created
Each suite tests: resolved payload → alert updated, no new incident
Each suite tests: duplicate payload → deduplication (update, not create)
Each suite tests: malformed payload → 400 error response
CloudWatch: test SNS SubscriptionConfirmation handling
CloudWatch: test SNS signature validation (valid and invalid)
Generic: test HMAC-SHA256 signature verification
Generic: test auto-generated external_id deduplication
Cross-source test: same alert from Prometheus and Grafana → two separate alerts (different source)
Realistic sample payloads stored in docs/examples/ for each source
All tests pass without external service dependencies (mocked HTTP)
tasks.implementation_notes: Use the same test pattern as prometheus_webhook_test.go: in-memory SQLite, full HTTP handler stack. Store sample payloads as testdata fixtures.

tasks.name: Webhook provider documentation and example payloads
tasks.description: Document all webhook endpoints, payload formats, and setup instructions for each monitoring source
acceptance_criteria: docs/webhooks.md created with setup guide for each source
Grafana section: alerting contact point configuration, webhook URL, example payload
CloudWatch section: SNS topic setup, subscription creation, IAM permissions, example payload
Generic section: schema reference, HMAC signing, curl examples
docs/examples/ updated with sample payloads: grafana-firing.json, cloudwatch-alarm.json, generic-alert.json
docs/API.md updated with all four webhook endpoints
README.md updated with multi-source support in features section
tasks.implementation_notes: Good documentation is critical for adoption. Users need step-by-step guides for connecting their monitoring tools to OpenIncident.

external: Grafana 9+ Unified Alerting webhook format documentation
AWS SNS message format and signature verification documentation
AWS SDK for Go (SNS signature verification)
